claims.statement: Invalid Module, Contract, Claim, Gate, authority, and assurance references are rejected.
A compiled Context Capsule cannot grant read or write scope broader than its primary Module, declared Contracts, and focused verification context.

Given an exact Project Model and tracked-path observation, the ownership compiler validates canonical exact-or-recursive-prefix Module and ungoverned disposition selectors, rejects every latent or concrete owner-owner, owner-disposition, or disposition-disposition overlap without precedence, deterministically assigns every tracked path to exactly one Module write owner or one explicit ungoverned disposition, and fails closed on a missing assignment.

Generated Context Capsule write scope is projected only from an opaque, exact-Model-and-path-facts-bound ownership product for the primary Module; absent, forged, stale, ambiguous, disposition-only, or over-limit input fails closed.

Given a frozen Project Model, its opaque exact path-ownership product, a current Context Session projection, and requested concrete paths, Project Model derives the exact newly disclosed path owners, Contract relations, Assurance crossings, disposition references, and Context Impact digest; worker or caller supplied owner, Module, Contract, assurance, semantic-impact, or verification classifications cannot substitute those facts.

The fixed ownership proof rejects unowned tracked paths, overlapping Module write owners, owner/disposition conflicts, malformed disposition rules, forged or stale ownership products, and Context Capsule scope derivation that bypasses the product.

The fixed versioned app/apk/relay reference uses the ordinary Project Model, path-ownership, assurance-boundary, Contract, Plan, and Context Capsule compilers without a schema exception: relay is governed, app and apk are independent provisional public-Contract providers, legacy remains explicitly opaque and covered by one non-authorizing ungoverned disposition, every tracked path has exactly one assignment, and the relay's initial compiled read scope contains its own paths plus exact governance and dependency Contract surfaces but no app, apk, or legacy implementation path.
Gate Evidence for one Claim cannot cover another Claim without explicit semantics and an authority-bound Verification Obligation.

When plan alignment is required, an ordinary Change can compile only against active Development Outcomes in its frozen Governance Baseline; plan amendments are authority-bound and isolated from implementation.

Given Project Model and Change record inputs, the Architecture Acceptance Profile compiler produces a deterministic bounded projection that preserves exact references and relations across Outcome, Criterion, Claim, Gate, Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap; it never represents an Outcome Contribution as Criterion satisfaction.

The Project Model treats blocker relations as separate monotonic closure-consumption constraints, validates canonical append-only blocksGapClosureRefs and exact blocksOutcomeTransitionRoutes declared by open Knowledge Gaps that already own governed Closure Contracts, and rejects unknown or already-closed Gap targets, invalid Outcome routes, duplicates, self-edges, cycles, prior-relation removal or mutation, additions after source closure, and mixed declaration with a named closure or matching Transition. For each candidate Outcome status delta it derives the exact Gap disposition set from frozen Criterion Gaps, matching baseline-open Outcome-route blockers, and every required Gap's transitive baseline-open blockers; a blocker clears only when its own unchanged open-to-closed Closure Contract proof compiles atomically in that Transition, never from a status edit alone.

From one compiler-produced opaque Claim Gate route product bound to the exact unchanged Project Model, Project Model compiles under one aggregate bounded budget the complete canonical Claim descriptors visible through each requested Module's publicContracts and dependency via Contracts, plus the complete acceptance-eligible original Gate and command routes for those Claims and explicitly selected Module/Claim pairs. Explicit selections may address frozen-Plan target or cross-source Claims but never widen authoring visibility; routes admitted only by integration- or release-only full-Gate expansion are excluded; original route shape, order, and canonical digest are preserved; and forged, wrong-Model, content-drifted, incomplete-coverage, malformed, unknown-Module, or over-limit inputs fail closed without requiring a consumer to traverse Contracts or reinterpret Gate scope, command scope, or effective Module applicability.

Before activation, a planned or conditional Outcome definition may change only through one bounded append-only Revision whose previous complete normalized definition and digest match the frozen Governance Baseline, whose current digest matches the candidate Plan, whose changed fields are compiler-derived, and whose rationale and required Plan authority are exact; removal, rewrite, reordering, no-op, fork, or Revision beside a status Transition fails closed.

Outcome activation binds the latest frozen-baseline definition digest and rejects activation in the same Amendment as a Revision; once active, achieved, or retired, the complete normalized Outcome definition is immutable.

An Outcome Revision does not change lifecycle status, an Amendment that appends any Revision cannot append any Outcome status Transition, and a Revision cannot authorize its own Plan Amendment or any ordinary Change created before the Revision is Accepted into a later frozen Governance Baseline.

The fixed Project Model Outcome Revision proof rejects a missing, deleted, reordered, rewritten, forked, no-op, or digest-inconsistent Revision and accepts only exact complete prior and current definitions for a planned or conditional Outcome.

The fixed Project Model Outcome Revision proof rejects any Amendment that appends both a Revision and an Outcome status Transition, any active or terminal definition drift, and activation not bound to the exact latest frozen-baseline definition.
